chunks.text: Gene Luen Yang wins MacArthur Genius' Grant - EECS at Berkeley EECS alumnus Gene Luen Yang (CS B.A. ’95) has been selected for a MacArthur fellowship, one of the most prestigious prizes in the United States. Awarded for exceptional “originality, insight and potential, ” a MacArthur prize comes with a no-strings-attached grant of $625,000 distributed over five years. In January, Gene became the first graphic novelist named national ambassador for children’s literature by the Library of Congress. Categories Alumni Honors View Open Faculty Positions
